mission.title: Install and Configure Cluster API (CAPI)
mission.description: Cluster API (CAPI) is a Kubernetes operator that provides declarative APIs and tooling to simplify the provisioning, upgrading, and management of multiple Kubernetes clusters. It is particularly useful for platform operators looking to automate cluster lifecycle management across various infrastructure environments. Key trade-offs include the need for a deeper understanding of Kubernetes concepts and potential complexity in setup compared to simpler solutions.

steps.title: Add the Cluster API Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official Cluster API Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add cluster-api https://charts.cluster-api.sigs.k8s.io

```

> This is the official repository for Cluster API.

steps.title: Install the Cluster API CRDs
steps.description:
This step installs the Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) required for Cluster API to function.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/cluster-api/releases/download/v1.12.4/cluster-api-components.yaml

```

> This YAML file contains all necessary CRDs.

steps.title: Install Cluster API components
steps.description:
This step installs the Cluster API components using Helm, which will manage the lifecycle of your clusters.

```bash

helm install cluster-api cluster-api/cluster-api --namespace kube-system --version 1.12.4

```

> Ensure you are installing in the kube-system namespace.

steps.title: Create a Cluster API management cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a management cluster that will be used to manage other clusters.

```bash

kubectl create cluster --name my-management-cluster --kubernetes-version v1.27.0

```

> Replace 'my-management-cluster' with your desired cluster name.

steps.title: Verify Cluster API installation
steps.description:
This step checks if the Cluster API components are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n kube-system

```

> You should see the Cluster API components running.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Cluster API components
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Cluster API components and CRDs. Be aware that this may result in data loss for managed clusters.

```bash

helm uninstall cluster-api --namespace kube-system

kubectl delete -f https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/cluster-api/releases/download/v1.12.4/cluster-api-components.yaml

```

upgrade.title: Upgrade Cluster API components
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you backup your cluster state. This step will upgrade the Cluster API components to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get clusters -o yaml > backup-clusters.yaml

helm upgrade cluster-api cluster-api/cluster-api --namespace kube-system --version 1.12.4

kubectl get pods -n kube-system

```

> Backup your current cluster state.
> Upgrade to the specified version.
> Verify that the pods are running after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'no matches for kind "Cluster" in version "cluster.x-k8s.io/v1alpha4"'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the Cluster CRD is not installed or not recognized.

**Fix:**
Reapply the CRDs using the command: kubectl apply -f https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/cluster-api/releases/download/v1.12.4/cluster-api-components.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'failed to create cluster: unable to create control plane'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the control plane components are not properly configured.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the control plane pods using: kubectl logs -l control-plane=cluster-api -n kube-system

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'unable to connect to the server: x509: certificate signed by unknown authority'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates an issue with the kubeconfig or the cluster's certificate.

**Fix:**
Ensure your kubeconfig is correctly set up and points to the right cluster context.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'no resources found in kube-system namespace'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the Cluster API components are not installed.

**Fix:**
Reinstall the components using the Helm install command provided in the installation steps.

versionNotes.changes: Improved support for custom infrastructure providers and enhanced error handling.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated the old API version 'v1alpha3' for Cluster and Machine resources.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v1.11.x, ensure to migrate your existing clusters to the new API version.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get pods -n kube-system' showing all Cluster API components in a Running state.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with sufficient resources.
